docs.title: Class: Concept
docs.text:
Concept that serves as a member of a semantic object. This is intended to be used for defining the permissible values of an OmopSemanticObject, such as the members of an OmopGroup or OmopEnum. The concept_id and label slots are used to specify the concept_id and label of the concept that serves as a member of the enumeration.

__

URI: https://example.org/omop_named_sets/Concept

    click Concept href "../Concept/"

      Concept : label

docs.title: Class: OmopEnum
docs.text:
Enumeration (static) of permissible values for a particular slot. This is intended to be used for defining slots that have a fixed set of permissible values, such as the staging axis (T, N, M, Group). This will not update dynamically with vocabulary updates, so should be used for concepts that are short lists and not expected to change over time.

__

URI: https://example.org/omop_named_sets/OmopEnum

docs.title: Class: OmopGroup
docs.text:
Named group of OMOP concepts defined by their membership in a particular group, such as hierarchy and / or domain. Importantly, this is not a static definition and if the vocabularies are updated, the membership of these groups may change. This is intended to be used for defining sets of concepts that are used in semantic definitions, such as the set of all T stage concepts.

__

URI: https://example.org/omop_named_sets/OmopGroup

  and if the vocabularies are updated, the membership of these groups may change.
  This is intended to be used for defining sets of concepts that are used in semantic
  definitions, such as the set of all  T stage concepts.

  '

docs.title: Class: OmopSemanticObject
docs.text:
Abstract base class for OMOP semantic objects. Used to represent concepts, groups of concepts, enumerations of concepts, and value sets of concepts in the context of defining complex semantic structures for the OMOP CDM.

__

docs.title: Class: OmopValueSet
docs.text:
A semantic grouping of mixed types of permissible values for a template slot. Members may be OmopConcepts, OmopGroups, or OmopEnums. This represents a registry-level value domain, not a direct OMOP structure.

docs.title: Class: Concept
docs.text:
The Standardized Vocabularies contains records, or Concepts, that uniquely identify each fundamental unit of meaning used to express clinical information in all domain tables of the CDM. Concepts are derived from vocabularies, which represent clinical information across a domain (e.g. conditions, drugs, procedures) through the use of codes and associated descriptions. Some Concepts are designated Standard Concepts, meaning these Concepts can be used as normative expressions of a clinical entity within the OMOP Common Data Model and standardized analytics. Each Standard Concept belongs to one Domain, which defines the location where the Concept would be expected to occur within the data tables of the CDM. Concepts can represent broad categories (‘Cardiovascular disease’), detailed clinical elements (‘Myocardial infarction of the anterolateral wall’), or modifying characteristics and attributes that define Concepts at various levels of detail (severity of a disease, associated morphology, etc.). Records in the Standardized Vocabularies tables are derived from national or international vocabularies such as SNOMED-CT, RxNorm, and LOINC, or custom OMOP Concepts defined to cover various aspects of observational data analysis.

URI: omop_cdm54:Concept

      Concept : concept_name

      Concept : domain_id





        Concept --> "1" Domain : domain_id
        click Domain href "../Domain/"

      Concept : standard_concept

      Concept : valid_end_date

      Concept : valid_start_date

      Concept : vocabulary_id





        Concept --> "1" Vocabulary : vocabulary_id
        click Vocabulary href "../Vocabulary/"

docs.title: Direct
docs.text:
name: Concept
description: The Standardized Vocabularies contains records, or Concepts, that uniquely
  identify each fundamental unit of meaning used to express clinical information in
  all domain tables of the CDM. Concepts are derived from vocabularies, which represent
  clinical information across a domain (e.g. conditions, drugs, procedures) through
  the use of codes and associated descriptions. Some Concepts are designated Standard
  Concepts, meaning these Concepts can be used as normative expressions of a clinical
  entity within the OMOP Common Data Model and standardized analytics. Each Standard
  Concept belongs to one Domain, which defines the location where the Concept would
  be expected to occur within the data tables of the CDM. Concepts can represent broad
  categories (‘Cardiovascular disease’), detailed clinical elements (‘Myocardial infarction
  of the anterolateral wall’), or modifying characteristics and attributes that define
  Concepts at various levels of detail (severity of a disease, associated morphology,
  etc.). Records in the Standardized Vocabularies tables are derived from national
  or international vocabularies such as SNOMED-CT, RxNorm, and LOINC, or custom OMOP
  Concepts defined to cover various aspects of observational data analysis.
from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
attributes:
  concept_id:
    name: concept_id
    description: A unique identifier for each Concept across all domains.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    identifier: true
    domain_of:
    - ConceptSynonym
    - Concept
    required: true
  concept_name:
    name: concept_name
    description: An unambiguous, meaningful and descriptive name for the Concept.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    required: true
  domain_id:
    name: domain_id
    description: A foreign key to the DOMAIN table the Concept belongs to.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    - Domain
    range: Domain
    required: true
  vocabulary_id:
    name: vocabulary_id
    description: A foreign key to the VOCABULARY table indicating from which source
      the Concept has been adapted.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    - Vocabulary
    range: Vocabulary
    required: true
  concept_class_id:
    name: concept_class_id
    description: The attribute or concept class of the Concept. Examples are ‘Clinical
      Drug’, ‘Ingredient’, ‘Clinical Finding’ etc.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConceptClass
    - Concept
    range: ConceptClass
    required: true
  standard_concept:
    name: standard_concept
    description: This flag determines where a Concept is a Standard Concept, i.e.
      is used in the data, a Classification Concept, or a non-standard Source Concept.
      The allowable values are ‘S’ (Standard Concept) and ‘C’ (Classification Concept),
      otherwise the content is NULL.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
  concept_code:
    name: concept_code
    description: The concept code represents the identifier of the Concept in the
      source vocabulary, such as SNOMED-CT concept IDs, RxNorm RXCUIs etc. Note that
      concept codes are not unique across vocabularies.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    required: true
  valid_start_date:
    name: valid_start_date
    description: The date when the Concept was first recorded. The default value is
      1-Jan-1970, meaning, the Concept has no (known) date of inception.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConceptRelation
    - Concept
    required: true
  valid_end_date:
    name: valid_end_date
    description: The date when the Concept became invalid because it was deleted or
      superseded (updated) by a new concept. The default value is 31-Dec-2099, meaning,
      the Concept is valid until it becomes deprecated.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConceptRelation
    - Concept
    required: true
  invalid_reason:
    name: invalid_reason
    description: Reason the Concept was invalidated. Possible values are D (deleted),
      U (replaced with an update) or NULL when valid_end_date has the default value.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConceptRelation
    - Concept

docs.text:
name: Concept
description: The Standardized Vocabularies contains records, or Concepts, that uniquely
  identify each fundamental unit of meaning used to express clinical information in
  all domain tables of the CDM. Concepts are derived from vocabularies, which represent
  clinical information across a domain (e.g. conditions, drugs, procedures) through
  the use of codes and associated descriptions. Some Concepts are designated Standard
  Concepts, meaning these Concepts can be used as normative expressions of a clinical
  entity within the OMOP Common Data Model and standardized analytics. Each Standard
  Concept belongs to one Domain, which defines the location where the Concept would
  be expected to occur within the data tables of the CDM. Concepts can represent broad
  categories (‘Cardiovascular disease’), detailed clinical elements (‘Myocardial infarction
  of the anterolateral wall’), or modifying characteristics and attributes that define
  Concepts at various levels of detail (severity of a disease, associated morphology,
  etc.). Records in the Standardized Vocabularies tables are derived from national
  or international vocabularies such as SNOMED-CT, RxNorm, and LOINC, or custom OMOP
  Concepts defined to cover various aspects of observational data analysis.
from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
attributes:
  concept_id:
    name: concept_id
    description: A unique identifier for each Concept across all domains.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    identifier: true
    alias: concept_id
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - ConceptSynonym
    - Concept
    range: string
    required: true
  concept_name:
    name: concept_name
    description: An unambiguous, meaningful and descriptive name for the Concept.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    alias: concept_name
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: string
    required: true
  domain_id:
    name: domain_id
    description: A foreign key to the DOMAIN table the Concept belongs to.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    alias: domain_id
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    - Domain
    range: Domain
    required: true
  vocabulary_id:
    name: vocabulary_id
    description: A foreign key to the VOCABULARY table indicating from which source
      the Concept has been adapted.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    alias: vocabulary_id
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    - Vocabulary
    range: Vocabulary
    required: true
  concept_class_id:
    name: concept_class_id
    description: The attribute or concept class of the Concept. Examples are ‘Clinical
      Drug’, ‘Ingredient’, ‘Clinical Finding’ etc.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    alias: concept_class_id
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - ConceptClass
    - Concept
    range: ConceptClass
    required: true
  standard_concept:
    name: standard_concept
    description: This flag determines where a Concept is a Standard Concept, i.e.
      is used in the data, a Classification Concept, or a non-standard Source Concept.
      The allowable values are ‘S’ (Standard Concept) and ‘C’ (Classification Concept),
      otherwise the content is NULL.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    alias: standard_concept
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: string
  concept_code:
    name: concept_code
    description: The concept code represents the identifier of the Concept in the
      source vocabulary, such as SNOMED-CT concept IDs, RxNorm RXCUIs etc. Note that
      concept codes are not unique across vocabularies.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    alias: concept_code
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: string
    required: true
  valid_start_date:
    name: valid_start_date
    description: The date when the Concept was first recorded. The default value is
      1-Jan-1970, meaning, the Concept has no (known) date of inception.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    alias: valid_start_date
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - ConceptRelation
    - Concept
    range: string
    required: true
  valid_end_date:
    name: valid_end_date
    description: The date when the Concept became invalid because it was deleted or
      superseded (updated) by a new concept. The default value is 31-Dec-2099, meaning,
      the Concept is valid until it becomes deprecated.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    alias: valid_end_date
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - ConceptRelation
    - Concept
    range: string
    required: true
  invalid_reason:
    name: invalid_reason
    description: Reason the Concept was invalidated. Possible values are D (deleted),
      U (replaced with an update) or NULL when valid_end_date has the default value.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    alias: invalid_reason
    owner: Concept
    domain_of:
    - ConceptRelation
    - Concept
    range: string

docs.title: Class: ConceptAncestor
docs.text:
The CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table is designed to simplify observational analysis by providing the complete hierarchical relationships between Concepts. Only direct parent-child relationships between Concepts are stored in the CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP table. To determine higher-level ancestry connections, all individual direct relationships would have to be navigated at analysis time. The CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table includes records for all parent-child relationships, as well as grandparent-grandchild relationships and those of any other level of lineage for Standard or Classification concepts. Using the CONCEPT_ANCESTOR table allows for querying for all descendants of a hierarchical concept, and the other way around. For example, drug ingredients and drug products, beneath them in the hierarchy, are all descendants of a drug class ancestor. This table is entirely derived from the CONCEPT, CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP, and RELATIONSHIP tables.

URI: omop_cdm54:ConceptAncestor

 classDiagram
    class ConceptAncestor
    click ConceptAncestor href "../ConceptAncestor/"
      ConceptAncestor : ancestor_concept_id





        ConceptAncestor --> "1" Concept : ancestor_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      ConceptAncestor : descendant_concept_id





        ConceptAncestor --> "1" Concept : descendant_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      ConceptAncestor : max_levels_of_separation

      ConceptAncestor : min_levels_of_separation

docs.title: Class: ConceptClass
docs.text:
The CONCEPT_CLASS table includes semantic categories that reference the source structure of each Vocabulary. Concept Classes represent so-called horizontal (e.g. MedDRA, RxNorm) or vertical levels (e.g. SNOMED) of the vocabulary structure. Vocabularies without any Concept Classes, such as HCPCS, use the vocabulary_id as the Concept Class. This reference table is populated with a single record for each Concept Class, which includes a Concept Class ID and a fully specified Concept Class name.

URI: omop_cdm54:ConceptClass

 classDiagram
    class ConceptClass
    click ConceptClass href "../ConceptClass/"
      ConceptClass : concept_class_concept_id





        ConceptClass --> "1" Concept : concept_class_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      ConceptClass : concept_class_id

      ConceptClass : concept_class_name

docs.title: Slots

docs.title: Class: ConceptRelation
docs.text:
The CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP table contains records that define relationships between any two Concepts and the nature or type of the relationship. This table captures various types of relationships, including hierarchical, associative, and other semantic connections, enabling comprehensive analysis and interpretation of clinical concepts. Every kind of relationship is defined in the RELATIONSHIP table.

URI: omop_cdm54:ConceptRelation

 classDiagram
    class ConceptRelation
    click ConceptRelation href "../ConceptRelation/"
      ConceptRelation : concept_id_1





        ConceptRelation --> "1" Concept : concept_id_1
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      ConceptRelation : concept_id_2





        ConceptRelation --> "1" Concept : concept_id_2
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      ConceptRelation : invalid_reason

      ConceptRelation : relationship_id





        ConceptRelation --> "1" Relationship : relationship_id
        click Relationship href "../Relationship/"



      ConceptRelation : valid_end_date

      ConceptRelation : valid_start_date

docs.title: Slots
docs.text: Name Cardinality and Range Description Inheritance concept_id_1 1  Concept direct concept_id_2 1  Concept direct relationship_id 1  Relationship The relationship between CONCEPT_ID_1 and CONCEPT_ID_2 direct valid_start_date 1  String The date when the relationship is first recorded direct valid_end_date 1  String The date when the relationship is invalidated direct invalid_reason 0..1  String Reason the relationship was invalidated direct

docs.title: Class: ConceptSynonym
docs.text:
The CONCEPT_SYNONYM table captures alternative terms, synonyms, and translations of Concept Name into various languages linked to specific concepts, providing users with a comprehensive view of how Concepts may be expressed or referenced.

URI: omop_cdm54:ConceptSynonym

 classDiagram

    click ConceptSynonym href "../ConceptSynonym/"

        ConceptSynonym --> "1" Concept : concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      ConceptSynonym : concept_synonym_name

      ConceptSynonym : language_concept_id





        ConceptSynonym --> "1" Concept : language_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"

docs.title: Class: ConditionOccurrence
docs.text:
REWRITE THIS This table captures records about the exposure to a Drug ingested or otherwise introduced into the body. A Drug is a biochemical substance formulated in such a way that when administered to a Person it will exert a certain biochemical effect on the metabolism. Drugs include prescription and over-the-counter medicines, vaccines, and large-molecule biologic therapies. Radiological devices ingested or applied locally do not count as Drugs.

URI: omop_cdm54:ConditionOccurrence

docs.text:
name: ConditionOccurrence
description: REWRITE THIS This table captures records about the exposure to a Drug
  ingested or otherwise introduced into the body. A Drug is a biochemical substance
  formulated in such a way that when administered to a Person it will exert a certain
  biochemical effect on the metabolism. Drugs include prescription and over-the-counter
  medicines, vaccines, and large-molecule biologic therapies. Radiological devices
  ingested or applied locally do not count as Drugs.
from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
attributes:
  condition_occurrence_id:
    name: condition_occurrence_id
    description: The unique key given to a condition record for a person.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    identifier: true
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    required: true
  person_id:
    name: person_id
    description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the condition is recorded.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
    - ObservationPeriod
    - Person
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: Person
    required: true
  condition_concept_id:
    name: condition_concept_id
    description: The CONDITION_CONCEPT_ID field is recommended for primary use in
      analyses, and must be used for network studies. This is the standard concept
      mapped from the source value which represents a condition
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    range: Concept
    required: true
  condition_start_date:
    name: condition_start_date
    description: Use this date to determine the start date of the condition
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    required: true
  condition_start_datetime:
    name: condition_start_datetime
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
  condition_end_date:
    name: condition_end_date
    description: Use this date to determine the end date of the condition
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
  condition_end_datetime:
    name: condition_end_datetime
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
  condition_type_concept_id:
    name: condition_type_concept_id
    description: This field can be used to determine the provenance of the Condition
      record, as in whether the condition was from an EHR system, insurance claim,
      registry, or other sources.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    range: Concept
    required: true
  condition_status_concept_id:
    name: condition_status_concept_id
    description: This concept represents the point during the visit the diagnosis
      was given (admitting diagnosis, final diagnosis), whether the diagnosis was
      determined due to laboratory findings, if the diagnosis was exclusionary, or
      if it was a preliminary diagnosis, among others.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    range: Concept
  stop_reason:
    name: stop_reason
    description: The Stop Reason indicates why a Condition is no longer valid with
      respect to the purpose within the source data. Note that a Stop Reason does
      not necessarily imply that the condition is no longer occurring.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
  provider_id:
    name: provider_id
    description: The provider associated with condition record, e.g. the provider
      who made the diagnosis or the provider who recorded the symptom.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
    - Person
    - VisitOccurrence
  visit_occurrence_id:
    name: visit_occurrence_id
    description: The visit during which the condition occurred.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: VisitOccurrence
  visit_detail_id:
    name: visit_detail_id
    description: The VISIT_DETAIL record during which the condition occurred. For
      example, if the person was in the ICU at the time of the diagnosis the VISIT_OCCURRENCE
      record would reflect the overall hospital stay and the VISIT_DETAIL record would
      reflect the ICU stay during the hospital visit.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
  condition_source_value:
    name: condition_source_value
    description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data epresenting
      the condition that occurred. For example, this could be an ICD10 or Read code.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
  condition_source_concept_id:
    name: condition_source_concept_id
    description: This is the concept representing the condition source value and may
      not necessarily be standard. This field is discouraged from use in analysis
      because it is not required to contain Standard Concepts that are used across
      the OHDSI community, and should only be used when Standard Concepts do not adequately
      represent the source detail for the Condition necessary for a given analytic
      use case. Consider using CONDITION_CONCEPT_ID instead to enable standardized
      analytics that can be consistent across the network.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    range: Concept
  condition_status_source_value:
    name: condition_status_source_value
    description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing
      the condition status.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence

docs.title: Class: Domain
docs.text:
The DOMAIN table includes a list of OMOP-defined Domains to which the Concepts of the Standardized Vocabularies can belong. A Domain represents a clinical definition whereby we assign matching Concepts for the standardized fields in the CDM tables. For example, the Condition Domain contains Concepts that describe a patient condition, and these Concepts can only be used in the condition_concept_id field of the CONDITION_OCCURRENCE and CONDITION_ERA tables. This reference table is populated with a single record for each Domain, including a Domain ID and a descriptive name for every Domain.

URI: omop_cdm54:Domain

 classDiagram
    class Domain
    click Domain href "../Domain/"
      Domain : domain_concept_id





        Domain --> "1" Concept : domain_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Domain : domain_id

      Domain : domain_name

Drugs include prescription and over-the-counter medicines, vaccines, and large-molecule biologic therapies. Radiological devices ingested or applied locally do not count as Drugs.

docs.title: Direct
docs.text:
name: DrugExposure
description: This table captures records about the exposure to a Drug ingested or
  otherwise introduced into the body. A Drug is a biochemical substance formulated
  in such a way that when administered to a Person it will exert a certain biochemical
  effect on the metabolism. Drugs include prescription and over-the-counter medicines,
  vaccines, and large-molecule biologic therapies. Radiological devices ingested or
  applied locally do not count as Drugs.
from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
attributes:
  drug_exposure_id:
    name: drug_exposure_id
    description: The unique key given to records of drug dispensings or administrations
      for a person.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    identifier: true
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
    required: true
  person_id:
    name: person_id
    description: The PERSON_ID of the PERSON for whom the drug dispensing or administration
      is recorded.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
    - ObservationPeriod
    - Person
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: Person
    required: true
  drug_concept_id:
    name: drug_concept_id
    description: This is the standard concept mapped from the source concept id which
      represents a drug product or molecule otherwise introduced to the body.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
    range: Concept
    required: true
  drug_exposure_start_date:
    name: drug_exposure_start_date
    description: Start date of a prescription, the date a prescription was filled,
      or the date on which a Drug administration was recorded.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
    required: true
  drug_exposure_start_datetime:
    name: drug_exposure_start_datetime
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  drug_exposure_end_date:
    name: drug_exposure_end_date
    description: The DRUG_EXPOSURE_END_DATE denotes the day the drug exposure ended
      for the patient.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
    required: true
  drug_exposure_end_datetime:
    name: drug_exposure_end_datetime
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  verbatim_end_date:
    name: verbatim_end_date
    description: This is the end date of the drug exposure as it appears in the source
      data, if it is given
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  drug_type_concept_id:
    name: drug_type_concept_id
    description: You can use the TYPE_CONCEPT_ID to delineate between prescriptions
      written vs. prescriptions dispensed vs. medication history vs. patient-reported
      exposure, etc.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
    range: Concept
    required: true
  stop_reason:
    name: stop_reason
    description: The reason a person stopped a medication as it is represented in
      the source.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
  refills:
    name: refills
    description: This is only filled in when the record is coming from a prescription
      written this field is meant to represent intended refills at time of the prescription.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  quantity:
    name: quantity
    description: If liquid, quantity stands for the total amount dispensed or ordered
      of ingredient. For clinical drugs with fixed dose forms (tablets etc.) the quantity
      is the number of units/tablets/capsules prescribed or dispensed (can be partial,
      but then only 1/2 or 1/3, not 0.01). Clinical drugs with divisible dose forms
      (injections) the quantity is the amount of ingredient the patient got.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  days_supply:
    name: days_supply
    description: The number of days of supply of the medication as recorded in the
      original prescription or dispensing record.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  sig:
    name: sig
    description: This is the verbatim instruction for the drug as written by the provider.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  route_concept_id:
    name: route_concept_id
    description: The standard CONCEPT_ID that the ROUTE_SOURCE_VALUE maps to in the
      route domain.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
    range: Concept
  lot_number:
    name: lot_number
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  provider_id:
    name: provider_id
    description: The Provider associated with drug record, e.g. the provider who wrote
      the prescription or the provider who administered the drug.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
    - Person
    - VisitOccurrence
  visit_occurrence_id:
    name: visit_occurrence_id
    description: The Visit during which the drug was prescribed, administered or dispensed.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: VisitOccurrence
  visit_detail_id:
    name: visit_detail_id
    description: The VISIT_DETAIL record during which the drug exposure occurred.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
  drug_source_value:
    name: drug_source_value
    description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing
      the drug exposure that occurred.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  drug_source_concept_id:
    name: drug_source_concept_id
    description: This is the concept representing the drug source value and may not
      necessarily be standard.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
    range: Concept
  route_source_value:
    name: route_source_value
    description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing
      the drug route.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure
  dose_unit_source_value:
    name: dose_unit_source_value
    description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing
      the dose unit of the drug given.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - DrugExposure

docs.title: Class: Location
docs.text:
The LOCATION table represents a generic way to capture physical location or address information of Persons and Care Sites.

URI: omop_cdm54:Location

 classDiagram
    class Location
    click Location href "../Location/"
      Location : address_1

      Location : address_2

      Location : city

      Location : country_concept_id





        Location --> "0..1" Concept : country_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Location : country_source_value

      Location : county

      Location : latitude

      Location : location_id

      Location : location_source_value

      Location : longitude

      Location : state

      Location : zip

docs.title: Class: Note
docs.text:
The NOTE table captures unstructured information that was recorded by a provider about a patient in free text (in ASCII, or preferably in UTF8 format) notes on a given date. The type of note_text is CLOB or varchar(MAX) depending on RDBMS.

URI: omop_cdm54:Note

 classDiagram
    class Note
    click Note href "../Note/"
      Note : encoding_concept_id





        Note --> "1" Concept : encoding_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Note : language_concept_id





        Note --> "1" Concept : language_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Note : note_class_concept_id





        Note --> "1" Concept : note_class_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Note : note_date

      Note : note_datetime

      Note : note_event_field_concept_id





        Note --> "0..1" Concept : note_event_field_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Note : note_event_id

      Note : note_id

      Note : note_source_value

      Note : note_text

      Note : note_title

      Note : note_type_concept_id





        Note --> "1" Concept : note_type_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Note : person_id





        Note --> "1" Person : person_id
        click Person href "../Person/"



      Note : provider_id

      Note : visit_detail_id

      Note : visit_occurrence_id





        Note --> "0..1" VisitOccurrence : visit_occurrence_id
        click VisitOccurrence href "../VisitOccurrence/"

docs.title: Class: ObservationPeriod
docs.text:
This table contains records which define spans of time during which two conditions are expected to hold (i) Clinical Events that happened to the Person are recorded in the Event tables, and (ii) absence of records indicate such Events did not occur during this span of time.

URI: omop_cdm54:ObservationPeriod

 classDiagram
    class ObservationPeriod
    click ObservationPeriod href "../ObservationPeriod/"
      ObservationPeriod : observation_period_end_date

      ObservationPeriod : observation_period_id

      ObservationPeriod : observation_period_start_date

      ObservationPeriod : period_type_concept_id





        ObservationPeriod --> "1" Concept : period_type_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      ObservationPeriod : person_id





        ObservationPeriod --> "1" Person : person_id
        click Person href "../Person/"

    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Person
    range: Concept
    required: true

docs.title: Class: Relationship
docs.text:
The RELATIONSHIP table provides a reference list of all types of relationships that can be used to associate any two Concepts in the CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP table, the respective reverse relationships, and their hierarchical characteristics. Note, that Concepts representing relationships between the clinical facts, used for filling in the FACT_RELATIONSHIP table are stored in the CONCEPT table and belong to the Relationship Domain.

URI: omop_cdm54:Relationship

 classDiagram

    click Relationship href "../Relationship/"
      Relationship : defines_ancestry

      Relationship : is_hierarchical

      Relationship : relationship_concept_id





        Relationship --> "1" Concept : relationship_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Relationship : relationship_id

      Relationship : relationship_name

      Relationship : reverse_relationship_id

docs.title: Class: VisitOccurrence
docs.text:
This table contains Events where Persons engage with the healthcare system for a duration of time. They are often also called “Encounters”. Visits are defined by a configuration of circumstances under which they occur, such as (i) whether the patient comes to a healthcare institution, the other way around, or the interaction is remote, (ii) whether and what kind of trained medical staff is delivering the service during the Visit, and (iii) whether the Visit is transient or for a longer period involving a stay in bed.

URI: omop_cdm54:VisitOccurrence

docs.title: Direct
docs.text:
name: VisitOccurrence
description: This table contains Events where Persons engage with the healthcare system
  for a duration of time. They are often also called “Encounters”. Visits are defined
  by a configuration of circumstances under which they occur, such as (i) whether
  the patient comes to a healthcare institution, the other way around, or the interaction
  is remote, (ii) whether and what kind of trained medical staff is delivering the
  service during the Visit, and (iii) whether the Visit is transient or for a longer
  period involving a stay in bed.
from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
attributes:
  visit_occurrence_id:
    name: visit_occurrence_id
    description: Use this to identify unique interactions between a person and the
      health care system. This identifier links across the other CDM event tables
      to associate events with a visit.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    identifier: true
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
    - VisitOccurrence
    required: true
  person_id:
    name: person_id
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
    - ObservationPeriod
    - Person
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: Person
    required: true
  visit_concept_id:
    name: visit_concept_id
    description: This field contains a concept id representing the kind of visit,
      like inpatient or outpatient. All concepts in this field should be standard
      and belong to the Visit domain.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: Concept
    required: true
  visit_start_date:
    name: visit_start_date
    description: For inpatient visits, the start date is typically the admission date.
      For outpatient visits the start date and end date will be the same.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
    required: true
  visit_start_datetime:
    name: visit_start_datetime
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
  visit_end_date:
    name: visit_end_date
    description: For inpatient visits the end date is typically the discharge date.
      If a Person is still an inpatient in the hospital at the time of the data extract
      and does not have a visit_end_date, then set the visit_end_date to the date
      of the data pull.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
    required: true
  visit_end_datetime:
    name: visit_end_datetime
    description: If a Person is still an inpatient in the hospital at the time of
      the data extract and does not have a visit_end_datetime, then set the visit_end_datetime
      to the datetime of the data pull.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
  visit_type_concept_id:
    name: visit_type_concept_id
    description: Use this field to understand the provenance of the visit record,
      or where the record comes from.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: Concept
    required: true
  provider_id:
    name: provider_id
    description: There will only be one provider per visit record and the ETL document
      should clearly state how they were chosen (attending, admitting, etc.). If there
      are multiple providers associated with a visit in the source, this can be reflected
      in the event tables (CONDITION_OCCURRENCE, PROCEDURE_OCCURRENCE, etc.) or in
      the VISIT_DETAIL table.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - ConditionOccurrence
    - DrugExposure
    - Note
    - Person
    - VisitOccurrence
  care_site_id:
    name: care_site_id
    description: This field provides information about the Care Site where the Visit
      took place.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    domain_of:
    - Person
    - VisitOccurrence
  preceding_visit_occurrence_id:
    name: preceding_visit_occurrence_id
    description: Use this field to find the visit that occurred for the person prior
      to the given visit. There could be a few days or a few years in between.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: VisitOccurrence
  visit_source_value:
    name: visit_source_value
    description: This field houses the verbatim value from the source data representing
      the kind of visit that took place (inpatient, outpatient, emergency, etc.)
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
  visit_source_concept_id:
    name: visit_source_concept_id
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: Concept
  admitted_from_concept_id:
    name: admitted_from_concept_id
    description: Use this field to determine where the patient was admitted from.
      This concept is part of the visit domain and can indicate if a patient was admitted
      to the hospital from a long-term care facility, for example.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: Concept
  admitted_from_source_value:
    name: admitted_from_source_value
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
  discharged_to_concept_id:
    name: discharged_to_concept_id
    description: Use this field to determine where the patient was discharged to after
      a visit. This concept is part of the visit domain and can indicate if a patient
      was transferred to another hospital or sent to a long-term care facility, for
      example. It is assumed that a person is discharged to home therefore there is
      not a standard concept id for “home”. Use concept id = 0 when a person is discharged
      to home.
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence
    range: Concept
  discharged_to_source_value:
    name: discharged_to_source_value
    from_schema: https://timformatie.github.io/SEIN-OMOP/omop-cdm54
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - VisitOccurrence

docs.title: Class: Vocabulary
docs.text:
The VOCABULARY table includes a list of the Vocabularies integrated from various sources or created de novo in OMOP CDM. This reference table contains a single record for each Vocabulary and includes a descriptive name and other associated attributes for the Vocabulary.

URI: omop_cdm54:Vocabulary

 classDiagram
    class Vocabulary
    click Vocabulary href "../Vocabulary/"
      Vocabulary : vocabulary_concept_id





        Vocabulary --> "1" Concept : vocabulary_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Vocabulary : vocabulary_id

      Vocabulary : vocabulary_name

      Vocabulary : vocabulary_reference

      Vocabulary : vocabulary_version

docs.title: Slot: condition_source_concept_id
docs.text:
This is the concept representing the condition source value and may not necessarily be standard. This field is discouraged from use in analysis because it is not required to contain Standard Concepts that are used across the OHDSI community, and should only be used when Standard Concepts do not adequately represent the source detail for the Condition necessary for a given analytic use case. Consider using CONDITION_CONCEPT_ID instead to enable standardized analytics that can be consistent across the network.

URI: omop_cdm54:condition_source_concept_id Alias: condition_source_concept_id

docs.title: Slot: discharged_to_concept_id
docs.text:
Use this field to determine where the patient was discharged to after a visit. This concept is part of the visit domain and can indicate if a patient was transferred to another hospital or sent to a long-term care facility, for example. It is assumed that a person is discharged to home therefore there is not a standard concept id for “home”. Use concept id = 0 when a person is discharged to home.

docs.title: Slot: quantity
docs.text:
If liquid, quantity stands for the total amount dispensed or ordered of ingredient. For clinical drugs with fixed dose forms (tablets etc.) the quantity is the number of units/tablets/capsules prescribed or dispensed (can be partial, but then only 1/2 or 1/3, not 0.01). Clinical drugs with divisible dose forms (injections) the quantity is the amount of ingredient the patient got.

docs.title: Class: ConceptClass
docs.text:
The CONCEPT_CLASS table includes semantic categories that reference the source structure of each Vocabulary. Concept Classes represent so-called horizontal (e.g. MedDRA, RxNorm) or vertical levels (e.g. SNOMED) of the vocabulary structure. Vocabularies without any Concept Classes, such as HCPCS, use the vocabulary_id as the Concept Class. This reference table is populated with a single record for each Concept Class, which includes a Concept Class ID and a fully specified Concept Class name.

URI: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept/ConceptClass

    click ConceptClass href "../ConceptClass/"

        click Concept href "../Concept/"

      ConceptClass : concept_class_name

docs.title: Class: Domain
docs.text:
The DOMAIN table includes a list of OMOP-defined Domains to which the Concepts of the Standardized Vocabularies can belong. A Domain represents a clinical definition whereby we assign matching Concepts for the standardized fields in the CDM tables. For example, the Condition Domain contains Concepts that describe a patient condition, and these Concepts can only be used in the condition_concept_id field of the CONDITION_OCCURRENCE and CONDITION_ERA tables. This reference table is populated with a single record for each Domain, including a Domain ID and a descriptive name for every Domain.

URI: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept/Domain

 classDiagram
    class Domain
    click Domain href "../Domain/"
      Domain : domain_concept_id





        Domain --> "1" Concept : domain_concept_id
        click Concept href "../Concept/"



      Domain : domain_id

      Domain : domain_name

docs.title: Class: Relationship
docs.text:
The RELATIONSHIP table provides a reference list of all types of relationships that can be used to associate any two Concepts in the CONCEPT_RELATIONSHIP table, the respective reverse relationships, and their hierarchical characteristics. Note, that Concepts representing relationships between the clinical facts, used for filling in the FACT_RELATIONSHIP table are stored in the CONCEPT table and belong to the Relationship Domain.

URI: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept/Relationship

docs.text:
The VOCABULARY table includes a list of the Vocabularies integrated from various sources or created de novo in OMOP CDM. This reference table contains a single record for each Vocabulary and includes a descriptive name and other associated attributes for the Vocabulary.

docs.title: omop-cdm
docs.text: LinkML version of OMOP CDM including OMOP-tuned convention enforcement

docs.title: Class: Concept
docs.text:
The Standardized Vocabularies contains records, or Concepts, that uniquely identify each fundamental unit of meaning used to express clinical information in all domain tables of the CDM. Concepts are derived from vocabularies, which represent clinical information across a domain (e.g. conditions, drugs, procedures) through the use of codes and associated descriptions. Some Concepts are designated Standard Concepts, meaning these Concepts can be used as normative expressions of a clinical entity within the OMOP Common Data Model and standardized analytics. Each Standard Concept belongs to one Domain, which defines the location where the Concept would be expected to occur within the data tables of the CDM. Concepts can represent broad categories ('Cardiovascular disease'), detailed clinical elements ('Myocardial infarction of the anterolateral wall'), or modifying characteristics and attributes that define Concepts at various levels of detail (severity of a disease, associated morphology, etc.). Records in the Standardized Vocabularies tables are derived from national or international vocabularies such as SNOMED-CT, RxNorm, and LOINC, or custom OMOP Concepts defined to cover various aspects of observational data analysis.

URI: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept/Concept

 classDiagram
    class Concept
    click Concept href "../Concept/"
      Concept : concept_class_id





        Concept --> "1" ConceptClass : concept_class_id
        click ConceptClass href "../ConceptClass/"



      Concept : concept_code

      Concept : concept_id

      Concept : concept_name

      Concept : domain_id





        Concept --> "1" Domain : domain_id
        click Domain href "../Domain/"



      Concept : invalid_reason

      Concept : standard_concept





        Concept --> "0..1" standard_concept : standard_concept
        click standard_concept href "../standard_concept/"



      Concept : valid_end_date

      Concept : valid_start_date

      Concept : vocabulary_id





        Concept --> "1" Vocabulary : vocabulary_id
        click Vocabulary href "../Vocabulary/"

docs.title: Direct
docs.text:
name: Concept
description: The Standardized Vocabularies contains records, or Concepts, that uniquely
  identify each fundamental unit of meaning used to express clinical information in
  all domain tables of the CDM.  Concepts are derived from vocabularies, which represent
  clinical information across a domain (e.g. conditions, drugs, procedures) through
  the use of codes and associated descriptions.  Some Concepts are designated Standard
  Concepts, meaning these Concepts can be used as normative expressions of a clinical
  entity within the OMOP Common Data Model and standardized analytics.  Each Standard
  Concept belongs to one Domain, which defines the location where the Concept would
  be expected to occur within the data tables of the CDM.  Concepts can represent
  broad categories ('Cardiovascular disease'), detailed clinical elements ('Myocardial
  infarction of the anterolateral wall'), or modifying characteristics and attributes
  that define Concepts at various levels of detail (severity of a disease, associated
  morphology, etc.).  Records in the Standardized Vocabularies tables are derived
  from national or international vocabularies such as SNOMED-CT, RxNorm, and LOINC,
  or custom OMOP Concepts defined to cover various aspects of observational data analysis.
from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
attributes:
  concept_id:
    name: concept_id
    description: Unique identifier for the concept in the OMOP CDM.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    identifier: true
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: integer
    required: true
  concept_name:
    name: concept_name
    description: An unambiguous, meaningful and descriptive name for the Concept.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: string
    required: true
  concept_code:
    name: concept_code
    description: The concept code represents the identifier of the Concept in the
      source vocabulary, such as SNOMED-CT concept IDs, RxNorm RXCUIs etc. Note that
      concept codes are not unique across vocabularies.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: string
    required: true
  domain_id:
    name: domain_id
    description: A foreign key to the [DOMAIN](https://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm54.html#domain)
      table the Concept belongs to.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    - Domain
    range: Domain
    required: true
  vocabulary_id:
    name: vocabulary_id
    description: A foreign key to the [VOCABULARY](https://ohdsi.github.io/CommonDataModel/cdm54.html#vocabulary)
      table indicating from which source the Concept has been adapted.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    - Vocabulary
    range: Vocabulary
    required: true
  concept_class_id:
    name: concept_class_id
    description: The attribute or concept class of the Concept. Examples are 'Clinical
      Drug', 'Ingredient', 'Clinical Finding' etc.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    - ConceptClass
    range: ConceptClass
    required: true
  standard_concept:
    name: standard_concept
    description: This flag determines where a Concept is a Standard Concept, i.e.
      is used in the data, a Classification Concept, or a non-standard Source Concept.
      The allowable values are 'S' (Standard Concept) and 'C' (Classification Concept),
      otherwise the content is NULL.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: standard_concept
    required: false
  valid_start_date:
    name: valid_start_date
    description: The date when the Concept was first recorded. The default value is
      1-Jan-1970, meaning, the Concept has no (known) date of inception.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: date
    required: true
  valid_end_date:
    name: valid_end_date
    description: The date when the Concept became invalid because it was deleted or
      superseded (updated) by a new concept. The default value is 31-Dec-2099, meaning,
      the Concept is valid until it becomes deprecated.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: date
    required: true
  invalid_reason:
    name: invalid_reason
    description: Reason the Concept was invalidated. Possible values are D (deleted),
      U (replaced with an update) or NULL when valid_end_date has the default value.
    from_schema: https://athena.ohdsi.org/search-terms/terms#concept
    rank: 1000
    domain_of:
    - Concept
    range: string
    required: false

docs.title: Slot: standard_concept
docs.text:

docs.title: Slot: valid_end_date
docs.text:
The date when the Concept became invalid because it was deleted or superseded (updated) by a new concept. The default value is 31-Dec-2099, meaning, the Concept is valid until it becomes deprecated.
